reviews.review_text:
I used to love this game and have spent countless nights where I should have been studying, ruling the star systems with friends. However I am so tired of taking a break, coming back and having to relearn the game because they rework how the game runs and functions every month or so. Very few times have these changes actually improved the game, many break it. 

I understand it's paradox, but the DLC are insane, I have most of them and can that most of them should have been built into the main game for free. 

Last but not least.. The User data collection and selling of such data to third parties is such a low blow, especially for a company that charges you $40 for a base game and $160 for DLC afterwards. The data collection is invasive, unnecessary, and downright greedy. 

If I could, I wish I could return the game, DLC, and get my life back that I wasted with this game

The game is currently rated very positive for all time reviews, and mixed for recent reviews. Let me explain what's happening.

Game good. Game get update. Update causes loads of lag and very bug-ridden. Fanbase justifiably upset. Mixed reviews.

That being said, they have been working on addressing these issues since and the game runs better now. Stellaris has recently hit its 10-year anniversary and with this game getting constant free updates and new content since I think that while I agree the production team isn't without criticism the game is still really, really high quality.

Like all paradox games, this is a scam meant to sell you DLC. The base game is a hollow shell. I paid for 1 month subscription to all the DLC and I can tell you that none of it is worth your cold hard cash. They sell you almost nothing in each DLC. It only looks like a lot of content spread out over 10 years of bloated development. They still demand retail prices for new DLC and season passes. The next DLC they promise ships that function like planets. Sounds cool but not worth paying money for it. If Paradox was a good company, they would have stopped developing this game and made Stellaris 2. But the quality in their games have spiraled into the gutter and all they care about is milking DLC. All the veterans are mad about changes and it's clear the game's balance is ruined with each new patch. They censor forums, ban dissenting opinions, and have armies of shills that will berate you for any criticism. Once you have played a complete game or two, everything becomes boring and the events are no longer interesting.

I cant play the game anymore, the new update made it impossible for my launcher to even open the game without my antivirus program stopping it from going up.

You know they got alot of bad spyware in the game when even windows security has to step in.

Good game, BUT !

+ + + A very good simulation of diplomacy, empire building, resource and population management and a bit of spaceship combat as well. Was lots of fun building a galactic empire with my little brother together. Overall a good strategy game. 

- - - Note that 70% of the content what this game can to offer is locked behind DLC paywalls. OVER 100€ WORTH OF DLC ON SALE.... Lates DLC is 25€... Alternative option is the monthly sub for a 10 years old game. 
Too much greed and not enough focus on the development on the gameplay.

One comment I was reading in a forum and completely agree with: 
""I have the impression that the Paradox game developers are under a strong constant pressure to add new features into the game, so that Paradox can sell more DLC. Due to this, Paradox games are constantly being flooded with large amounts of half-baked new features, which is hurting the core gameplay of its games. I really wish that Paradox would spend more time polishing existing features and fixing bugs, and would be more careful about adding new features to the game, because these new features often upset the delicate balance of the game.""

- Get the base game on sale and if you like it then add some DLC you like.
But be aware, this game is designed that the more you play the more DLC content you wanna see.

This game has gone through so many reworks that it is barely the same game I bought at launch. Unfortunately this has not made Stellaris better, instead it has made it into Frankenstein's Game with serious balance and performance issues. Also it has now received so many DLCs that it a subscription option just to make it affordable. Hold onto your wallets because paradox is coming for them like a space pirate.
The developers seem hell-bent on ruining this once great game, just make a new game and stop breaking and downgrading existing features.
i love this game but **** what the **** is wrong with paradox why are you still dropping dlc bull**** when your ******* multiplayer doesn't  even work either fix it or drop a new  stellaris

Sadly, after so long of really loving and enjoying this game, I am gonna have to write this. (I have played the game since Hyperlanes were an option, not the given)

It really is a shell of its former self in terms of what it feels like to play, 5 years ago, every game you had felt unique and different, and this is with there being LESS content then there currently is.

This is because that, despite the many times more content in the game now then before, the quality has been dropping farther and farther. Each big update leaves systems that have been forgotten, which there are plenty of, further out of date or outright obsoletes them.
Alongside that, part of what made each playthrough special was that you actually had time to take in what you were doing, and every decision you had had at least SOME effect on your empire, as well as the big events were rare, special, and made me want to start a new one over and over, just to see what happened.

Now? Youll click fast for 5 whole seconds, get 15 events, and half of them are just talking about how one of your scientists found another space plankton for 5% shield bonus, or your fourth repairable shipyard. the Marauders will squack at you for .000005% of your total GDP like they are actually a threat, the Khan will wake up and get killed by the closest fallen empire every time without fail.

Then it will go one of two ways: Youll either slog through 100 years of contsant emmisary requests, microing leaders as they die to ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, forgetting to check the galactic council because youre busy doing literally anything else and then a law ♥♥♥♥♥ you, so you leave and commit mass omnicide,

OR

Youll get bored of the same cycle of the game over and over again now by the Khan waking up that youll just stop playing.

TLDR: The way the game feels now makes it play more like the fortnight of 4x games then a story of the rise and fall of space empires, constant jingling keys and all.

SIDE NOTE: the DLC model is also ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ atrocious and now they have made like 60 dollars of DLC I paid for free and I got nothing for it, this shouldnt matter, but it tickles me wrong that I get shafted having paid for every DLC for years.
I love this game. you needed to play for like 20 hours to understand what to do. I love that I can rule the whole Galaxy and purge all the races. 10/10

I have over 300 hours of time playing the game. It was amazing but I just can't anymore. Paradox virtue signals whilst being the most greedy company that I know of. They charge what the base game should cost for AI slop DLC. They fundamentally change how the game plays so often, it just becomes too frustrating to keep investing time into. Paradox is like an evil version of Hello Games.

- terrible performance
- overpriced
- constant push of dlcs without which games lack 2/3 of content
- subscription
- it seems like they now push ai into their workflow as well
make your own mind, but i think this doesn't make case for buying this... uhm... opus

After 3000 hours into the previous versions of Stellaris and absolutely loving it, taking a long break, now trying it out again. Been recently playing it for around another 200 hours and I can with certainty, the reworks on pops and fleets is absolute unbearable. Pops now make no sense and you have to watch a freaking YouTube video to even understand it and even then it still roller-coasters for no reason. 

And the fleet rework... I lost a 68k fleet to a 38k startbase... do I need to say more.  If we are suppose to rock paper and scissor the Ai builds, then make it easier to do so and not the most expansive espionage operation. And AUTO GATHER INTEL!

As for now, I am done with this game. As someone who has bought all the DLCs and have over 3000 hours into it. It breaks my heart but this game as become garbage now.

Edit: after calming down, I can truely say no. The new fleet rework is just bad, they tried to break the "meta" ship building aspect but in doing so, forces the player to HAVE TO play by research enemy ships, which is just not worth the time and investment, then purposely build to counter it. It is taking away player agency because if you don't do that, you are not winning those fleet engagements. I have legitimate come to feel this game no longer respects my time.

ANOTHER case of "it wasn't broken and we fixed it anyway". 

The game was fine a couple years ago and they changed it with weird updates and made it worse and it sucks now. 

Also, their privacy policy is friggin orwellian, they're basically doing zuckerberg level data harvesting. Prepare to have the color of your butthole recorded and sold by these guys. 

Then there's the silly willy 300 bucks price, trying to gaslight you into buying a subscription. 

The game, a couple years ago, was a very good game. The game rn is a slightly below mediocre game. But the people who own this game have tried to turn this into such a shameless moneyfarm in all aspects that they just don't deserve your money.

This is the best terrible videogame I've ever played, or perhaps the worst 7/10 game I've ever enjoyed for cumulatively multiple hundreds of hours. 

Seen as a grand strategy game, Stellaris is a fascinating trainwreck that is slowly being improved at the cost of turning into a hyper-monetized walled garden that would make the likes of Blizzard Entertainment blush. Seen as a generator of emergent narrative, it is perhaps a masterpiece, but one buried beneath the second-clunkiest and most Byzantine user interface I've seen, surpassed in bewilderment only by the inimitable likes of Final Fantasy XIV. It's horrible. It's gorgeous. And the soundtrack absolutely rules. 

I wish I could get those couple hundred hours back, but at the same time, I regret nothing.
Can't get into it anymore. I used to love the game ~6 years ago, but between all the reworks and DLC bloat I just can't find the will to learn it one more time.
10 years of regulard DLC releases, countless forum threads, and the game still can't render text on 4K resolution in a way that does not hurt eyes.

I have it on gamepass for free.  AI balancing is awful.  Enemies spawning in fleets that they previously didn't have access to or the ability to muster all based on keeping the game going longer.  There are multiple instances I've encountered where the AI should have been in a state beyond repair yet somehow pulls out a large fleet within a month.  It makes for a very boring cat and mouse game.  The upgrade/micromanagement systems are pretty tedious.

Incredible game BUT : DLC price go brrr, it's good and they're not like dlc slop, it's good, well made, content-heavy DLCs but there are a lot of them, and because they're so conten heavy the base game seems so small without them, it's totally not the case btw, the base game is still really good, the dlcs are just that good, also my reccomendations for said dlcs 

Machine Age is a must if you like robots, Biogenesis is quite good, do reccomend, Lithoids is seen as bad by a lot of people but the species are just incredibly good looking, I use one of them half the time, Infernals is really good too, I would reccomend Nemesis in general but do not buy Cosmic storms or Distant Stars first, they're not as good at the others, I kinda just bought them for the cube species and the jellyfich amalgam species that you unlock when you have all the dlcs, also talking about species, create or loggin into your paradox account, the behemothkin species that you get from it is like in my top five species ever, and it goes well with Biogenesis for obvious reasons (the Behemoth)

also there is a mod workshop with a lot of good things, would reccomend Gigastructural Engineering if you like huge things (attack moon go brrr) or Ancient Cashe Of Technology (ACOT) if you like small but powerful tech, and both at the same time is kinda ridiculous, also the species mods by Silfae are really good, and there is two Star Trek total conversion mods, two Star Wars ones, and a Mass Effect one so a lot of potential, and the youtubers that play the game are generally really funny and the community is the opposite of toxic and kinda funny but on the good side of funny
Uses generative AI and aggressively harvests user data. Shameless while charging you more than £200 just for DLC. ♥♥♥♥ you.

This is a very complicated strategy game with a lot of depth. You will lose slowly and painfully over and over as you try to figure things out. There's nothing wrong with that if that's your cup of tea.

What's wrong with the game is that the Devs won't leave it the eff alone. DLC and expansions are great...but not if they rewrite the fundamental mechanics every time they want to introduce something new. I was never great at this game, but I could hold my own after a month or so of experimenting. After 100 hours in game, I can no longer play the game that I bought, and the dozens of changes are so disorienting I can't get any traction no matter what I do.

In short, I can't recommend this game unless it's going to be THE GAME you play for a while. If you're dedicated enough to keep up with the changes and master and re-master the systems, there's a lot to play with in here. Most of it's buried under layers of tedious complexity, but some people like that. I have no idea whether the devs are done mucking around, it's been years and they keep doing things to the game.
Great game way to expense dlc and 90% of the game is locked behind dlc that you either can subsrcibe to or pay a ungodly amount for

Man.... what have they done to this game.... They completely borked all sort of balance and diluted the game with too many overpriced DLC, and NOW go full generative AI?  Sad what state this once great game is in, uninstalling.
Fire, but needs cheaper DLCs. Especially because they basically make the game.

This Game feel like a cashgrab. every year, there's some '50$' worth of new (what ill affectionately describe as) 'Content', that keeps trying to update a ten year old engine to, at this point, make more money. Its going to keep increasing its price year-by-year, and that bothers me. as a *game* it is a wonderful game, with unmatched customization compared to the competition, (most of which is published by the same studio,) and yet it leaves me longing for something more in-depth. I like the game, but i wouldn't recommend it to anyone, (atleast anyone looking to buy all of it legitimately,) in this economy.
I cant believe they havent fixed the endgame yet. Game slows to 1 day in game to every 3 minutes. WHICH IS CRAZY. Im giving up on it now. ITs super frustrating, they should prioritze playability.

I liked the game, when it came out 10 years ago.  Now it is just a bunch of overpriced DLCs that are a mixed back of new mechanics and AI art and now with the Season "feature" of adding bundles of DLCs for what was the full price of the original price of the game is just too much IMO.  $376 USD for the current DLC is excessive.  Paying a monthly subscription to get access to the content without ever owning it is just as bad if not worse.  Paradox has a very bad habit of doing this with every game they out out.  Do not support this habit, tend, behavior (whatever you wanna call it).

A fantastic game with a very high level of customization. You can create virtually any type of empire you can think of. Has a few problems and dlc cost may be high but its totally worth it, or you can also pay the monthly subscription to have everything if you want to test it out.

I used to love Stellaris, I was in a group founded to play it in MP and as you can see I got over 1K in hours in the game, I have not played in over a year now and hardly in the year before that, every update I have seen since 3.0 seems to further break the game while more and more DLC comes out which changes or ruins the balance. Every couple of months my group considers playing, one of us boots up the game and looks and we all decide not to play. 

I cannot in good consciousness recommend this game given paradox's current model and the state of the game.

AI Generated Content Disclosure
The developers describe how their game uses AI Generated Content like this:

We employ generative AI technologies during the creation of some assets. Typically this involves the ideation of content and visual reference material. These elements represent a minor component of the overall development. AI has been used to generate voices for an AI antagonist and a player advisor.

It's not the game I purchased ten years ago, it's not even the game I played five years ago. Now it's got LLM-generated art, AI that blatantly cheat beyond even the spirit of higher difficulties. (They get no fog of war and can see you at all times just as an example) features introduced to the game as revolutionary are underpowered and useless (espionage) and ignored by the AI anyway.
Every time I play the game gets updated so far that my saves aren't valid, and it's not even easy to back-date the game to a previous point.
Game is alright, but the cost of DLCs that should just be content updates is unacceptable. Also generative AI :(

This game is a development mess. It is inconsistant and constantly broken, personaly, Ihave a feeling that devs justdon`t know what it shouldbe and constantly change directions instead of just picking one and follow it.

How you feel about Stellaris depends on your personal view on the DLC, your personal feelings about a game that changes over time, and your personal feelings toward use of generative AI.

If the latter is a dealbreaker, Stellaris is not a game you can play. They began using it around the Machine Age DLC, and do not appear to have walked it back since. They also have not doubled down on using it significantly more, that I have seen. Your own morals must determine where you stand on that line, but it is enough to give it a hard recommend against from me.

But if that's not a dealbreaker for you, consider the lack of consistency. Stellaris changes its face every two years, altering major game systems. When it released there were three forms of FTL travel. That was cut down significantly, with the other methods being added back piecemeal over time. Population was the gate for how well a game could run on weaker processors for half a decade, and at least two major reworks were designed to fix that specific issue. I do not know if it succeeded, in their most recent endeavor. This periodically breaks every mod that is made, and this is a game built on a strong modding community. The good news is that you CAN launch previous versions of the game without great issue; the unfortunate is that you must work much harder to have the right mods for the version of your choice, if it is not the most recent. Some will simply not be available, others requiring greater personal research and non-workshop methods.

If you don't like mods, however, that can be seen as a good thing. It's a game that has had consistent support for over a decade. It has also had consistent DLC. You can see the price easily enough, buying it all won't be cheap. And the rate at which things are released, and the lack of discounts over time, means that unless you're looking at a significant sale right at this moment you may very well get the best deal out of simply... getting the subscription. After all, they DO appear to still be releasing DLC currently, more than once a year. They elongate the timeframe that it is worthwhile even as you use it. And the DLC is very much an all-or-nothing deal; several introduce mechanics that other DLC expand on. Get only one, and you have a half-finished product. And with the updates to the base game, you will feel like you are missing out when returning later, even if you buy up to present and feel content there.

Stellaris is a game I loved. I want to support the people who made it happen. But I don't want to support the higher ups who would replace the people who made it happen with generative AI. So at this time, it's very hard to recommend, even with as much time as I have sunk into the game, and as much as I may yet sink in.
Buy to own games that are also somehow games as a service it truly one of the worst things paradox has come up with..

I have enjoyed this game A LOT in the past. Many long sessions with several people in big fun multiplayer sessions and this is what me and my friends primarily play stellaris for. The fun experience of a 4X/Grand strategy game like stellaris with the human diplomacy interaction. However the focus of paradox just like most of their other games has shifted. It is no longer about making sure that the game is good and stable and works and then release more content. It is 100% "Churn out meh content and charge lots for it" The old paradox is starting to look like they are dead. 

The result of the above is that you CANNOT currently play a multiplayer game even 40% of a "Whole session" as you will start getting desyncs every few seconds to the point that everyone will rage quit as it means you spend 20 seconds to resync everyone every 10-60 seconds (even with just 2-3 players) OR you might be able to do a full rehost of the save which takes 2-5 minutes to do and you MIGHT get another 5-15 minutes out of that before the cycle starts again with every few seconds.

Thanks to that i unfortunately in its current state have to give this a negative rating and it will not change until things change drasticly.
What was once fun, is now unplayable.

They bricked here be dragons in 4.3, no way to revert changes without reverting version of game

fix your slop paradox

The game itself is fun, and I enjoy it. But unfortunately, it follows an annoying trend in video games: it has a launcher. The funny thing is, the game runs great. But the launcher does not.

Sometimes it crashes, sometimes it freezes, sometimes it hangs. And it doesn't launch the game properly. It's built on Electron and the Chromium version it uses interacts poorly with Wayland.

Since the game doesn't launch, this review is going to have to be negative.

This has been a very fun game to go back to over and over again. Fighting for survival, managing your planets, and discovering new ones is a lot of fun. The space battles look pretty neat as well. It is super satisfying defending your worlds while picking them off from your nearest hostile neighbor. It is even better if you lean into diplomacy and gain a decent number of allies who help protect your territory. Overall, I'd definitely recommend trying this game out.

The AI is so bad the game is essentially unplayable, you cannot lose unless you up the difficulty by alot which is just giving the AI procentage modifers for everything to make up for it's inability to make fleets, good planets or ship designs.

They have irreversibly ruined the game and have zero concerns about it. Even worse performance, buggier, questionable gameplay & mechanical decisions, and subpar overpriced DLC they would rather spend time on than fixing & optimizing their broken game. This is all completely fine by Paradox's standards now. I am certainly not the first to say it and I won't be the last: the 4.0 update is one of the worst things Paradox has EVER done and they will never be able to patch the amount of holes they created at the rate they are currently going.

I used to enjoy playing a campaign every couple months. The new changes are frankly unfun. I've never seen literally an AI faction open the L-Gates so early, causing literally the entire galaxy to be wiped out by the Gray Tempest. I tried 3 different campaigns and none turned out well.

Game is still really good 
but once they started using Ai...  I don't support Ai

It's a great game but constantly asking $25-50 for new DLC, while also announcing the new DLC will be made with generative Ai, means they don't care about the product and just want to grab our money. Also the information it collects and shares without user knowledge, is invasive.

Classic Paradox DLC-slopmachine, with that classic Paradox lack of quality.

I remain amazed at this company's ability to turn their games into over an overgrown, under-optimized, buggy mess.

Stellaris was such a great game. Now it's just a half finished mess with 200€ dlc and season pass. If you won this game, you can roll back to the older versions. But I can't recommend buying this game, not anymore, which is a shame.

Lowkey this game has its problems, but I was able to mostly ignore them because I mostly just played small variations on the same tall trade and tech build every few months on singleplayer just to occupy myself and get comfortable with a video essay. However, with all the dlc stuff, and how updates keep upending how I like to play, I am honestly finding it hard to care enough to dip back into this game. Plus I frankly cant bring myself to recommend my friends put the same money and time into this game because I care about them too much asdjsdjd

Got a bug in one of the games, was banned while trying to create an account to report it on official forum.
Reporting bugs in this game is really burdensome.

While I have few hours on Steam I have been playing this game since 2020 on console and likely have hundreds if not thousands of hours there. This has got to be my favorite game of all time. Something about creating your own overarching sci fi story that lasts generations is something that most games do not have. Sadly I cannot recommed it due to the amount of predatory dlc and the new update. There was no reason for them to completely overhaul the games mechanics and the way economy works I was very happy with the gameplay loop they had and this new planetary system is just not good. The dlc while adding good content should have been mostly free updates. Please find something cheaper to spend you time on.

They recently pushed though a UI change that makes some elements off screen and unusable on steam deck.
And i cant just use the ui scaling bar, as the accept button is of screen

It takes a few play throughs and some wiki reading to get decent at.  Once you've got the basics down, the hours fly by.  Each expansion pack adds new twists and more play throughs.  I'm almost 200 hours in and still not tired of the game.

I have lost myself in this game so many times, it is so much fun to make a new civilization and guide them as they explore and establish who they are in the galaxy. If you like civilization or colony builder games you will adore this one.

Stellaris is still a good game, but it feels like it has drifted away from what originally made it special.

Many newer DLCs focus on highly specific fantasy-style narratives, scripted event chains and special mechanics, while traditional empire archetypes such as slavers, megacorps, hive minds and many older civics and ethics receive comparatively little attention. What once felt like emergent science fiction now often feels like a collection of pre-written adventures layered on top of a grand strategy game.

The development focus seems to have shifted from empire simulation towards narrative content. Regardless of the empire you start with, many games increasingly revolve around pursuing the same major progression systems and ascension paths. The defining feature of an empire often ends up being how it ascended rather than the ethics, civics or government it was founded upon.

An irony is that Paradox created the Custodian Team because players felt older mechanics were being left behind, yet many older civics, ethics and empire archetypes still feel neglected compared to the constant stream of new narrative systems.

Instead of another story-driven DLC, I'd rather see Paradox revisit older civics, ethics and empire archetypes so that a wider range of playstyles feels genuinely distinctive and worthwhile again. Stellaris doesn't need more stories. Unfortunately, the current DLC model appears far better suited to selling new narrative experiences than revisiting and strengthening the foundations that already exist.

this game is good, except for the ai slop they are now adding to it. i dont care if its only a small part of the game, this is shameful. i had been loving this game, and now to learn that its using ai slop? I'm disgusted, paradox, get your ♥♥♥♥ straight

I consider myself a role-play gamer.

Pros:

Interesting ideas about what a fresh civilization in the galaxy could encounter, like fallen empires, end-game crises, archaeology, Astral rifts, and all kinds of random events.

It provokes my thoughts about what economics and geopolitics can be in the interstellar era, and how high tech is not equal to high ethics. (I act like a Stalin-era command economy, so I thought it couldn't be in the real world. And you can still be Militarist, Xenophobe, or Imperial even if you can travel at light speed.)

It provides a basic idea of how war can be in the interstellar era. (It's basic because you cannot control like Homeworld when fights start.)

It contains all forms of civilization, like Necroids (if the fungus in The Last of Us had intelligence), Hive Minds (like that in Pluribus), and Machine Intelligence (like the Geth in Mass Effect).

Cons:

The AI empires are not smart; they close their borders for no reason, even if a fallen empire has awakened or an end-game crisis is at their door.

The economy in 4.3 is a disaster. I constantly encounter a resource deficit (after studying the videos), and the Class-3 and 4 Singularities are a joke! I have to mod the game and use the console to avoid getting overwhelmed by Growth Splinters.

Many things break the immersion, and you can only treat it as a numbers game; you cannot take it seriously.

I won't recommend this game, because I can't control my time put into this game. I thought I'd finish a small goal, but it took me hours. It frustrates me to find I still need lots of time to finish this game. Maybe it's because the economy in 4.3 makes me unable to sit and relax, maybe it's because it doesn't have a natural stopping point. I don't like this type of game, it makes me feel I didn't accomplish anything if I quit mid-game.

Paradox is a crime syndicate disguised as a game publisher. What do you mean I have to buy a relaunched version of Surviving Mars just to buy the DLC I already have? Even Skyrim isn't like that. Also they delisted Surviving Mars so this is the only place to publish a negative review that might actually be seen :/

I have never tried playing with more than 8 AI. but the game runs just fine as far as I can tell. The latest year I got too was 3460. graphics on medium settings. 

I am also curious. does anyone know if my PC could handle, Rome II total war or Warhammer 3 total war?

best way to describe it is galactic empire simulator but youre the emperor if you want to have your space empire like that or make it democratic or corpo or anything 
also runs good on mac but having trouble loading mods bc game crashes but vanilla game works great

So I've had Stellaris for quite a while now, it's really enjoyable even if you have to relearn it every year or so when an update comes out. The multiplayer is amazing provided you find someone else to play it with you (you won't), and all the story lines and side plots are really quite cute. 

Unfortunately the amount of DLC necessary to enjoy it to its full capacity is slightly ludicrous, especially when you haven't been slowly collecting them over the years. But that's not really a surprise for a paradox game.

Overall I'd definitely recommend giving it a go, just keep in mind that you'll probably spend more time thinking about playing this game than actually playing it.
Negative review just because of data collection strategy. Not going to play this game again (unfortunately I cannot refund it)
This game is great. I have logged time in both pc and Xbox Stellaris and i must say every time i play this game its something new.
A funny thing you can do aside from warcrimes is becoming a fanatic xenophile and making best friends with all the fanatic purifiers in the galaxy.
Extremely great roleplaying game and strategy game, you could write a full book about just one playthrough
This update seems poorly conceived and not a permanent solution. At this point, I don't even know how to play this game anymore. Keep trying, I believe, not all hope is lost for me personally.
Game crashes after install, tried a few fixes and still crashing.

Considering my hours, at the time of writing being about 700hrs, I hope I can say that I have a good understanding of the game and that I enjoy it. However, as much time I have spent playing, and not matter how much I enjoy it, to get all the DLC and content should not be over $200. There shouldn't be a Subscription Service as an option to enjoy all the content, solely because of the outragous prices. Especially for the amazing and fabled [Insert Species] Pack, that only provide different looks, and give no difference to gameplay, at the cost of $15 each!

Granted, there are some DLC's that are genuinely great, though once more not at the prices set. If any game represented the resounding issues present within Gaming. It would be seen clearly through Paradox, and particularly through Stellaris.

This game was incredibly fun a few years ago. I came back to the new update. I cannot recommend this game to new players. They really broke how fleets worked. Ships are very expensive but also now very weak; strong fleets regularly get wiped by weaker fleets and the interface doesn't give you any option to analyze why that happened. AI is vastly overpowered even on the lower difficulty settings. Pass on this one until Paradox fixes what they broke.

Each playthrough is a its own space opera. I haven't played in years and coming back to Stellaris and finding out that it is still developed and there's still new content (dlc) being created. 

Love this game!
its ight ig. main complaint is the sub based dlc or pay the ridiculous asking price.

Well, I played 1.0, and left when they changed to hyperlane-only in 2.0. It felt like a step back, but at least the AI seems to be able to handle this fairly well. Now I've recently started playing again, and although its not as good as it once was, its pretty wide in its scope and enjoyable for every now and again. It's still a paradox game, so don't expect much simulational depth, but a high complexity. 

For example if you play the synthetic fertility origin, where your population is sterile and so you need to find a mechanical way to grow your population again. You'll get a path to a solution with a few choices along the way; you wont get that problem and the tools to fix it somewhere in the game for you to piece together. Not entirely my preferance, this system of set origins and paths and dialog windows, but its not bad run either.

I never did this so figured I may as well.

The game has changed a LOT over the years, and I certainly had moments where I didn't enjoy playing it.

I wouldn't say it's a 10/10 but it's definitely one of the best space 4x ever. If you like space 4x games, it's hard to go wrong with Stellaris.

Greatest Space Strategy game of all time. It is not perfect in any way but strikes the right balance between micro management and macro management. 4.3 is the best version of the game yet, so try it out. The downside is the many DLC but Parador recently integrated 3 DLC into the base game, one of them was basically mandatory so its was a great move. Get the expansion pass for a month just to see wich DLC you like.

The game is the best attempt so far in gaming if you want to feel what being a galactic civilization is like. You can choose to be benevolent, ruthless, the defender of the galaxy everything. Then only caveat is that you're going to need many dlcs to do that, and they can easily reach 150-200 $ if you do not get them on sale, which is a shame.
Paradox games are all good. This is actually a colouring game. I really like it. How many magnificent cosmic epics occur in the blink of the stars. The mod community is also great.
Way too addicting, I get married in two months and I'm hoping I'll get bored of this game by then, otherwise it'll lead to my divorce.

Very in depth game with an awesome atmosphere and lots of customization. There are options on options to play the game. It can be a lot to chew at first but take your time and there is a lot to enjoy!

I have over 40 hours on this game. I like it when it works. Unfortunately, it almost never works. I have yet to actually finish a single game of Stellaris, because each time I have tried to start a game, I run into a game breaking bug that seems to be well-known in the community (or at least documented elsewhere online). 

For instance, I once had a single Empire (given the single same-colored realm of shared territory and shared name?) somehow spawn with two Homeworlds and different two leaders/governments? I had to go through first contact with them twice (given the two Homeworlds/governments), but everything else seemed shared between them, including intel, opinion, etc., ... after a bit of trying to continue the game (especially when they went to war), it just completely broke the galaxy.
Really fun but you have to learn it by playing more and more games and getting better reseaarch.
Have enjoyed the cahallenge altho sometimes the changes to the game through updates have a steep learning curve.
Great game, Played way too much. Insane replayability.

How to describe what Stellaris means to me. This is a game I cannot get out of my head as it has everything I love about gaming: fantastic stories, great challenges, science fiction, role playing aspects, great visuals and arguably the best game sound track ever made if not the best sound track full stop. The systems as always with grand strategy games are complex and intertwined, but I would argue that the tutorial mechanism of Stellaris is just enough of a nudge to start exploring the rest yourself, and boy does this game keep giving. Endless opportunities to build and roleplay an empire, and no run is the same as this game is an endless story generator with events, personal grudges, manoeuvring, warfare, the list never ends. At the time of writing I am almost 200 hours in and I feel I have barely scratched the surface and I still look forward to more. The game also has beautiful artwork ant writing, making it a joy to learn what is happening in your empire. Much is made of Paradox's DLC business model, but I must admit I really enjoy the new content as it gets released, allowing to play around with new factions, stories and mechanics. An underrated aspect for me is how well the Stellaris team creates the lore and background of their galaxies and expansions. Their expansion trailers are nothing short of astounding and the Stellaris chords at the end of every video are enough to draw me back for another game. Stellaris is my always game that I love playing on my steam deck and on the big screen and it will not be long before it becomes my most played game of all time. Stellaris is in a continuous fight with the Mass Effect Trilogy for the top spot in my top 10, and the best way I can phrase it is that if Mass Effect is my greatest game of all time then Stellaris is my favourite way to spend my gaming time. If you even remotely like sci fi or strategy gaming, this one is a must.
Incredible game, but its a paradox game, you need to buy some dlc's to finish the game's content.

Playing this game alone or with friends either way it's very fun. While I am not a fan of the DLC, if one of your friends has DLC installed you can play it with them. It's decently challenging but is one of the easier paradox games to understand.

This game has consistently been an anchor game for me, its constantly being updated, its dev's are constantly listening to its fanbase and there always seems to be a new way to play this game. I have spent a fortune keeping up with the DLC but honestly- worth every penny.

Some Notes:

1. the fleet rework and pop overhaul was tough to figure out, but its essentially relearning what i had to learn 500 hours ago. 
2. in the most recent change- the guardianship quest line is a little ridiculous- yeah its noble to give up your chair and then be gifted it back essentially but its end game after you beat the crisis??? but sure for the story line go off i guess.
It is a classic paradox slop dlc simulator.

Pretty good game but battles seem a bit lackluster and gameplay is very repetitive and limited I feel like.

One of the best games I have played, but no game is perfect, and Stellaris is not perfect.
Some things you should keep in mind before buying:
1. DLC adds a large amount of depth to the game, and that what can be considered "meta" is constantly changing, like how some mechanics from one dlc might be made redundant in the next update/dlc.
2. The AI, even after 10 years is still not good (ex. Fleets attacking starbases first in a system with hostile enemy fleets or occupying a planet in a existential campaign and surveying that system may be broken).
3. AI was used to make a hostile AI voice, an advisor voice, and artwork reference material, but not any outright generative AI artwork in the game.

Still a wonderful 4X game with lots of roleplay options and interesting player crisis paths, the lore goes crazy too.
9/10
Hard to learn at first but if you like, space, Aliens, and war this game is for you

The core game itself is great, all the issues come from Paradox's decisions be it updates or DLC, if you absolutely have to play it then I recommend to get the good ones, find any YT video they usually are right, and get it on a deep discount. Multiplayer depends heavily on how the game feels as sometimes I can play for a few hours no desync and other times we'd desync every minute. If Steam allowed a maybe for recommend I'd do that as some DLCs are great and required while others suck, also please stabilize the updates before pushing them.
